title: Erica phacelanthera
taxonomy: Erica phacelanthera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Erica, which is a large group of heath plants. Within the Erica genus, Erica phacelanthera is specifically classified as a member of the section Phyllodoceae. This section contains several species of Erica that are characterized by their distinctive flowers and growth habits. intro: Erica phacelanthera, also known as the bell heather, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the acidic soils of the heathlands in Europe. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Erica phacelanthera include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
